what_it_does: Stores an interpretive analysis of the deck1-2026 folder as a terse/presented cut of a shared source, cataloging confirmed design DNA, derived axes, and gaps routed to SYSTEM-GAPS.md.
observations: Frames deck1-2026 and pitch-deck as two cuts of one deck with shared DNA but different verbosity, motion, and length.
Includes a side-by-side register/purpose comparison table (terse/presented vs verbose/leave-behind).
Reuses the 0-9 grammar-delta ladder and confirm/new/generalise/contradict tag vocabulary.
Promotes the zoning ladder and gold ramp to CONFIRMED because exact values match across both folders.
Performs a deep comparative pass (sections A-I) that derives principles from the diff rather than either deck alone.
Declares deck1-2026 the canonical/living master and instructs sampling from it on conflict.
Routes all findings to SYSTEM-GAPS.md with explicit promotions.
standouts: Section A's space-time complexity trade-off: a slide's fixed complexity budget can be spent in static space or animation time.
Section B's rule that the pace-controller determines register: reader-paced artifacts are verbose/self-sufficient, presenter-paced artifacts are terse anchors.
Section E's {claim, support} bullet decomposition derived from the verbose-to-terse compression map.
Section D's invariant-skeleton-vs-flexible-muscle distinction: numbers and diagrams are sacred, prose is LOD-variable.
conventions: Evidence-grounded with exact hexes and measurements cited from the artifacts.
Two-folder confirmation discipline before promoting a property to CONFIRMED.
Axis-orthogonality treatment of register, LOD, and surface.
Token-driven routing of findings onto the existing --density knob and proposed motion/GIF slots.
Treats the diff between artifacts as primary data.
concerns: The canonical-master designation and 'sample from deck1-2026 on conflict' rule is an authority/ranking inference based on resolution and naming.
Several derived principles are extrapolated from a single 17-to-16 slide diff.
References SYSTEM-GAPS.md and a pitch-deck baseline whose contents are not visible in this file.
notes: A self-contained interpretive ledger entry that turns a verbose-vs-terse deck diff into a set of reusable generative axes and confirmation decisions.
questions: Which pitch-deck baseline analysis is being confirmed, and does it agree on the cited values?
Is the {claim, support} bullet content-model already implemented or only proposed here?
How is the canonical-master sampling rule reconciled with the stated no-ranking convention?
Are register, LOD, and motion-slot exposed as real data-* knobs or still listed in SYSTEM-GAPS.md?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — the stated purpose is a second-folder confirmation plus register/purpose analysis, and the body delivers the confirmation table, register comparison, and deep comparative derivation.
intention_signals: The author seems to be trying to demonstrate that one source deck can generate multiple outputs by separating invariant DNA from adjustable knobs, and to wire register, LOD, motion, and density as first-class generative controls in a single-source template engine.
novelty: The space-time complexity-budget framing, the pace-controller-determines-register rule, and the {claim, support} bullet decomposition derived from a compression map are unusual ways to model pitch-deck variants.
contribution: Contributes a worked example of deriving generative controls from artifact diffs; records the register/purpose axis, LOD-orthogonal-to-surface axis, motion/GIF slot placement rules, a {claim,support} bullet content-model, the invariant-skeleton-vs-flexible-muscle distinction, an ink-budget conservation rule, a space-time complexity-budget principle, and a two-folder confirmation discipline.
summary_for_embedding: An interpretive analysis JSON that compares two cuts of a pitch deck, confirms shared design DNA, and derives register, LOD, motion, and bullet-decomposition axes from the verbose-to-terse diff.
intention_for_embedding: To capture the principles and controls needed to treat one deck source as a multi-output generator with invariant DNA and adjustable verbosity, motion, and density knobs.
